content.thinking:
Let me implement the `findDuplicateOrderIds` function in TypeScript with tests. This is a straightforward implementation task.

The function needs to:
1. Find duplicate IDs (appear more than once)
2. Return empty array for no duplicates
3. Handle 1M items in under 100ms (needs O(n) approach using a Map/Set)
4. Return each duplicate ID exactly once
5. Handle empty array

O(n) approach: Use a Map to count occurrences, then filter for count > 1.

Let me check if there's an existing codebase structure first.

PASS .tmp/findDuplicateOrderIds.test.ts
  findDuplicateOrderIds
    ✓ AC1: returns IDs that appear more than once (2 ms)
    ✓ AC2: returns an empty array when no IDs are duplicated

    ✓ AC4: each duplicate ID appears exactly once even when it occurs 3+ times
    ✓ AC5: returns an empty array for an empty input
    ✓ returns an empty array for a single-element input
    ✓ returns a single entry when all IDs are identical
    ✓ returns all distinct duplicate IDs when several IDs are duplicated (1 ms)
